title: Implant extraction device
abstract: An extraction device for removing an implanted medical object, the device comprising: an elongated catheter body extending between a proximal end and a distal end along a longitudinal axis, the elongated catheter body defining an elongated aperture along the longitudinal axis, the elongated aperture being sized and shaped to receive the implanted medical object therein, the elongated catheter body further defining at least one waveguide receiving aperture extending between the proximal and distal ends of the elongated body; and at least one mechanical waveguide receivable in the at least one waveguide receiving aperture, the at least one mechanical waveguide extending between a proximal end operatively connectable to a mechanical wave generator and a distal end, the at least one mechanical waveguide configured to propagate at least one mechanical wave therealong.
claims: Claims 1. An extraction device for removing an implanted medical object, the device comprising: an elongated catheter body extending between a proximal end and a distal end along a longitudinal axis, the elongated catheter body defining an elongated aperture along the longitudinal axis, the elongated aperture being sized and shaped to receive the implanted medical object therein, the elongated catheter body further defining at least one waveguide receiving aperture extending between the proximal and distal ends of the elongated body; and at least one mechanical waveguide receivable in the at least one waveguide receiving aperture, the at least one mechanical waveguide extending between a proximal end operatively connectable to a mechanical wave generator and a distal end, the at least one mechanical waveguide configured to propagate at least one mechanical wave therealong. 2. The extraction device of claim 1, wherein the elongated catheter body comprises a first hollow and elongated body and a second hollow and elongated body, the second hollow and elongated body defining the elongated aperture for receiving the implanted medical object therein, the second hollow and elongated body being inserted into the first hollow and elongated body and a space between an external face of the second hollow and elongated body and an internal face of the first hollow and elongated body defines the at least one waveguide receiving aperture. 3. The extraction device of claim 2, wherein the first and second hollow and elongated bodies are tubular. 4. The extraction device of claim 3, wherein the first and second hollow and elongated bodies are concentric. 5. The extraction device of any one of claims 2 to 4, wherein a distal end of the first hollow and elongated body protrudes outwardly from a distal end of the second hollow and elongated body. 6. The extraction device of any one of claims 2 to 4, wherein a distal end of the second hollow and elongated body protrudes outwardly from a distal end of the first hollow and elongated body. 7. The extraction device of any one of claims 2 to 6, wherein the at least one mechanical waveguide comprises a plurality of mechanical waveguides each positioned within the space defined between an external face of the second hollow and elongated body and an internal face of the first hollow and elongated body. 8. The extraction device of claim 7, wherein the plurality of mechanical waveguides are positioned circumferentially about the second hollow and elongated body. 9. The extraction device of claim 8, wherein the plurality of mechanical waveguides are arranged as rows about the second hollow and elongated body. 10. The extraction device of claim 1, wherein the elongated catheter body is a solid elongated body provided with the elongated aperture and the at least one waveguide receiving aperture. 1 1. The extraction device of claim 10, wherein the at least one mechanical waveguide comprises plurality of mechanical waveguides and the at least one waveguide receiving aperture comprises a plurality of waveguide receiving apertures each for receiving a respective one of the plurality of mechanical waveguides. 12. The extraction device of claim 11 , wherein the plurality of waveguide receiving apertures are circumferentially positioned about the elongated aperture. 13. The extraction device of any one of claims 10 to 12, wherein a distal end of the solid elongated body is beveled. 14. The extraction device of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the at least one mechanical waveguide is longer than the elongated catheter body. 15. The extraction device of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the at least one mechanical waveguide is fixedly received in the at least one waveguide receiving aperture. 16. The extraction device of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the at least one mechanical waveguide is movably received in the at least one waveguide receiving aperture. 17. The extraction device of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the elongated aperture is sized and shaped to receive an electrical lead of a pacemaker therein. 18. The extraction device of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the elongated catheter body is made of a medical grade biocompatible material. 19. The extraction device of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the elongated catheter body is made of an acoustically insulating material. 20. The extraction device of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the at least mechanical waveguide is made of a medical grade biocompatible material. 21. A system for removing an implanted medical object, the device comprising: the extraction device of any one of claims 1 to 20, and a generator for generating mechanical waves, the generator being operatively connectable to the proximal end of the at least one mechanical waveguide for propagating the mechanical waves along the at least one mechanical waveguide. 22. The system of claim 21, wherein the generator is configured for generating mechanical pulses.
